# HSD 14027493926: [DMR A0] pCode is not well programming LP ratios when SST TF is enabled using UP 0x80000997

## Metadata

| Field | Value |

| Dimension | Value | Confidence |

**Reasoning**: No strong indicators found

## Root Cause Summary

BIOS: OKSDCRB1.86B.0033.D20.2603110927

uCode: 0x80000997

When SST TF is enabled pCode is 
not programming correctly
 the LP ratios on WP4:

Following table shows the ratio programed in sv.socket0.cbb0.base.punit_regs.punit_gpsb.gpsb_infvnn_io_regs.wp_cv_ia_ccp_wp4_0[0]

We expect LP ratios discovered in SST_TF_INFO_0 to be programmed in wp_cv_ia_ccp_wp4_0

MSR 198 is resolving wrong ratios due this.

We see this as a regression issue since it is correctly set in:

BIOS: OKSDREL1.86B.0032.D77.2

## Raw HSD Text

<!-- This section provides raw HSD data for agent enrichment (Stage 3b). -->
<!-- The Copilot agent extracts root cause, fix description, code refs, and diagrams. -->

### Description
BIOS: OKSDCRB1.86B.0033.D20.2603110927

uCode: 0x80000997

When SST TF is enabled pCode is 
not programming correctly
 the LP ratios on WP4:

Following table shows the ratio programed in sv.socket0.cbb0.base.punit_regs.punit_gpsb.gpsb_infvnn_io_regs.wp_cv_ia_ccp_wp4_0[0]

We expect LP ratios discovered in SST_TF_INFO_0 to be programmed in wp_cv_ia_ccp_wp4_0

MSR 198 is resolving wrong ratios due this.

We see this as a regression issue since it is correctly set in:

BIOS: OKSDREL1.86B.0032.D77.2602251015

uCode: 0x80000993

As expected, wp_cv_ia_ccp_wp4_0 is programmed with values from SST_TF_INFO_0

### Comments (latest)
++++14615256181 hmpicosm
Automated Message: This record has been cloned. The preceding comments are originally from the parent record 14027485938.

++++14615256546 lmalagon
Attached pCode vars dumps for both revisions.
++++1363614753 osumszyk
Hi, From the dump files attached I can see that: - in the 1st experiment with rev22.1.30.0, the SST PP level is 0: trl_manager.state.sst_pp_level=0x0 - in the 2nd experiment with rev22.1.30.2, the SST PP level is 2: trl_manager.state.sst_pp_level=0x2 The LP TRL limits are taken from: - TPMI register SST_TF_INFO_0 in fields LP_CLIP_RATIO_* for SST PP level 0 - TPMI register PP2_SST_TF_INFO_0 in fields LP_CLIP_RATIO_* for SST PP level 2 Thus, you compare the limits in WP4 with the limits for SST PP level 0, however in your experiment, the SST PP level is 2. From the dump files, I see the limits in WP4 correspond to the LP TRL limits for SST PP level 2: trl_manager.low_prio_clos_cdyn_trl.data.at0.value_=0x19 trl_manager.low_prio_clos_cdyn_trl.data.at1.value_=0x19 trl_manager.low_prio_clos_cdyn_trl.data.at2.value_=0xf trl_manager.low_prio_clos_cdyn_trl.data.at3.value_=0xa trl_manager.low_prio_clos_cdyn_trl.data.at4.value_=0x16 trl_manager.low_prio_clos_cdyn_trl.data.at5.value_=0x0 Orna
++++14615263953 lmalagon
We can close it as it is not a bug. Hi  @Sumszyk, Orna  Thanks for checking it, it was a confusion with the pp level as you mentioned. I double checked it and it is working as expected.
